topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. I stared at the screen, my breath catching in my throat, the cheerful colours of the notification mocking the sudden, crushing emptiness in my chest. "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise celebration for Alex has been cancelled." I reread the sentence three times. Unforeseen circumstances? What in the world did they mean? My hands started to tremble, the cheap plastic pen in my grip digging painfully into my palm. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet apartment. I paced the length of the living room, a tight, coiled energy vibrating beneath my skin. Every surface seemed too bright, too loud. I needed to call someone, anyone, but the silence of my own apartment was deafening, amplifying the frantic thumping in my ears.

[story 2]

I found the note tucked beneath the ceramic owl on the mantelpiece. The handwriting was neat, infuriatingly so, and the words confirmed my worst fears: the party was off. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck, turning my cheeks a deep crimson. I snatched my phone, my fingers flying across the screen as I scrolled through the group chat, each message a fresh wave of disbelief followed by a rising tide of sheer indignation. "Seriously?" I muttered, the word tasting bitter on my tongue. I threw my phone onto the sofa cushion, the impact barely registering over the churning sensation in my stomach. I stood up abruptly, kicking a stray magazine across the floor. The air felt thick, suffocating, and I wanted to scream until my throat was raw, but the sound caught somewhere behind my teeth, trapped by a rigid tension in my jaw.

[story 3]

When the text arrived, the world seemed to tilt on its axis. I reread the message, my eyes scanning the text as if searching for a hidden flaw, a typo that would somehow invalidate the entire situation. A low growl started deep in my chest, a rumble that I fought desperately to suppress. I walked into the kitchen, gripping the edge of the counter until my knuckles were white. The silence of the house was oppressive; the refrigerator hum sounded like a mocking judgment. I began pacing back and forth, a restless energy making my limbs feel too long, too twitchy. I ran a hand through my hair, pulling at the strands as if trying to physically pull the reality of the cancellation apart. A sharp, involuntary exhale escaped me, laced with pure, unadulterated frustration.
